message:
feat(runtime): add ShardedState for fine-grained locking (bd-3xvow)

Introduce ShardedState infrastructure to enable independent locking of
task, region, and obligation tables. This reduces contention by allowing
hot-path task operations to proceed without blocking region/obligation
mutations.

Key additions:
- ShardedConfig: read-only runtime config (Shard E)
- ShardedState: separate ContendedMutex for each shard (A/B/C)
- ShardGuard: type-safe multi-shard lock acquisition API
  - tasks_only() for hot-path poll operations
  - for_task_completed(), for_cancel(), for_obligation(), for_spawn()
  - all() for full-state operations

Lock order (per spec): E→D→B→A→C (Config→Instr→Regions→Tasks→Obligations)

Part of bd-2ijqf sharding work. Also includes remote_runtime_e2e test
improvements from concurrent work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2auz","title":"Filesystem E2E test suite: io_uring and directory operation tests","description":"# Filesystem E2E Test Suite

## Overview
Comprehensive end-to-end test suite for async filesystem operations covering
io_uring integration, directory operations, and cross-platform fallback behavior.

## Test Directory Structure
```
tests/e2e/fs/
├── mod.rs                       # Test module root
├── common/
│   ├── mod.rs                   # Shared utilities
│   ├── temp_dir.rs              # Temp directory management
│   └── fixtures.rs              # Test file fixtures
├── file/
│   ├── open_options.rs          # Open option combinations
│   ├── read_write.rs            # Basic read/write tests
│   ├── positional.rs            # read_at/write_at tests
│   ├── sync.rs                  # fsync/fdatasync tests
│   └── cancel.rs                # Cancellation tests
├── directory/
│   ├── create.rs                # mkdir tests
│   ├── remove.rs                # rmdir/unlink tests
│   ├── rename.rs                # rename tests
│   ├── readdir.rs               # Directory iteration
│   ├── metadata.rs              # stat tests
│   └── cancel.rs                # Cancellation tests
├── symlinks/
│   ├── create.rs                # symlink creation
│   └── readlink.rs              # symlink following
├── platform/
│   ├── io_uring.rs              # Linux io_uring path
│   └── fallback.rs              # Blocking pool fallback
└── lab/
    ├── deterministic.rs         # Lab runtime tests
    └── schedule_exploration.rs  # DPOR tests
```

## Test Categories

### 1. File Operation Tests
- Open with various flags (read, write, create, truncate, append)
- Sequential read/write
- Positional read_at/write_at
- Large file handling (100MB+)
- Sparse files
- fsync/fdatasync

### 2. Directory Operation Tests
- create_dir / create_dir_all
- remove_file / remove_dir / remove_dir_all
- rename (file and directory)
- ReadDir iteration
- metadata retrieval

### 3. Symlink Tests
- Create symbolic links
- Read symlink targets
- Follow symlinks in operations

### 4. Cancel-Correctness Tests
- Cancel during read/write
- Cancel during directory creation
- Cancel during recursive remove
- Region close with active operations

### 5. Platform Tests
- Verify io_uring path on Linux
- Verify blocking pool fallback on non-Linux
- Performance comparison

### 6. Lab Runtime Tests
- Deterministic file operations
- Virtual time for delays
- Schedule exploration

## Logging Requirements
- TRACE: Individual syscalls/SQEs
- DEBUG: Operation start/end
- INFO: Test progress, throughput
- WARN: Slow operations, partial writes
- ERROR: Failures with context

## Test Scripts

### Run All Filesystem Tests
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
export RUST_LOG="asupersync=debug,test=info"
export RUST_BACKTRACE=1

echo "=== Filesystem E2E Tests ==="
cargo test -p asupersync --test e2e_fs -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture 2>&1 | tee fs_tests.log

echo "=== Results ==="
grep -E '(PASS|FAIL|test result)' fs_tests.log
```

### Run Platform-Specific Tests
```bash
# Linux io_uring tests only
cargo test -p asupersync --test e2e_fs platform::io_uring:: -- --nocapture

# Fallback tests (simulated)
cargo test -p asupersync --test e2e_fs platform::fallback:: -- --nocapture
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All file operation tests pass
- [ ] All directory operation tests pass
- [ ] Symlink tests pass
- [ ] Cancel-correctness verified
- [ ] Platform tests verify io_uring on Linux
- [ ] Lab runtime tests deterministic
- [ ] Structured logging with artifacts
- [ ] CI integration

## Notes
Files are tested with temp directories that are cleaned up after each test.

 {"id":"bd-2c2i","title":"Lease manager: time-bounded resource claims","description":"# Lease Manager Implementation

## Goal

Implement a lease system for time-bounded claims on remote resources, preventing orphaned resources when nodes fail.

## Background

In distributed systems, resources can be orphaned when:
1. Client crashes without releasing resource
2. Network partition prevents release message
3. Client hangs indefinitely

Leases solve this: resources are only held for a bounded time. If not renewed, they're automatically released.

## Core Types

### Lease
```rust
pub struct Lease {
    /// Unique lease identifier
    id: LeaseId,
    
    /// Resource being leased
    resource: ResourceId,
    
    /// Holder of the lease
    holder: NodeId,
    
    /// When lease was granted
    granted_at: LogicalTime,
    
    /// When lease expires (must renew before)
    expires_at: LogicalTime,
    
    /// Lease state
    state: LeaseState,
}

pub enum LeaseState {
    Active,
    Expiring,  // Close to expiry, renewal needed
    Expired,   // Resource released
    Released,  // Holder explicitly released
}
```

### LeaseId
```rust
pub struct LeaseId {
    /// Resource ID
    resource: ResourceId,
    
    /// Unique grant nonce
    nonce: u64,
    
    /// Granting node
    grantor: NodeId,
}
```

## LeaseManager

```rust
pub struct LeaseManager {
    /// Active leases held by this node
    held: HashMap<LeaseId, Lease>,
    
    /// Leases granted by this node
    granted: HashMap<LeaseId, Lease>,
    
    /// Background renewal task
    renewal_task: TaskHandle,
    
    /// Clock source
    clock: Box<dyn LogicalClock>,
}

impl LeaseManager {
    /// Request a lease on a resource
    async fn acquire(&mut self, resource: ResourceId, duration: Duration) -> Result<Lease, LeaseError>;
    
    /// Renew an existing lease
    async fn renew(&mut self, lease: &mut Lease, duration: Duration) -> Result<(), LeaseError>;
    
    /// Release a lease early
    async fn release(&mut self, lease: Lease) -> Result<(), LeaseError>;
    
    /// Check if lease is still valid
    fn is_valid(&self, lease: &Lease) -> bool;
    
    /// Get time until expiry
    fn time_until_expiry(&self, lease: &Lease) -> Duration;
}
```

## Renewal Strategy

```rust
pub struct RenewalConfig {
    /// Renew when this fraction of lease time remains
    renewal_threshold: f64,  // e.g., 0.5 = renew at half-time
    
    /// Retry interval if renewal fails
    retry_interval: Duration,
    
    /// Max retries before giving up
    max_retries: u32,
}
```

Background task renews leases automatically:
1. Monitor all held leases
2. When time_until_expiry < threshold, renew
3. On failure, retry with backoff
4. If max_retries exceeded, trigger lease_lost callback

## Lease Lost Handling

When a lease expires without renewal:
```rust
pub trait LeaseObserver {
    /// Called when lease cannot be renewed
    fn on_lease_lost(&self, lease: LeaseId, reason: LeaseError);
}
```

Default behavior: cancel tasks/actors using the leased resource.

## Clock Considerations

Leases use logical time, not wall time:
- Prevents issues from clock skew
- Lease duration is 'messages' not 'seconds'
- Renewal piggybacks on normal communication

Alternative: Hybrid logical clocks for bounded skew tolerance.

## Wire Protocol

### LeaseRequest
```rust
struct LeaseRequest {
    resource: ResourceId,
    duration: Duration,
    holder: NodeId,
}
```

### LeaseResponse
```rust
enum LeaseResponse {
    Granted { lease: Lease },
    Denied { reason: LeaseDenied },
}

enum LeaseDenied {
    ResourceNotFound,
    AlreadyLeased { holder: NodeId, expires: LogicalTime },
    ResourceLimits,
}
```

## Integration Points

- RemoteHandle holds Lease for remote task
- ActorRef for remote actors has Lease
- Distributed regions use leases for membership
- Saga steps may acquire leases on resources

## Testing

- Lease acquisition and release
- Automatic renewal in background
- Lease expiry when holder crashes
- Multiple lease requests (conflict handling)
- Lab runtime with simulated network delays

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Lease and LeaseId types
- [ ] LeaseManager with acquire/renew/release
- [ ] Background renewal task
- [ ] LeaseState transitions
- [ ] LeaseObserver callback
- [ ] Wire protocol messages
- [ ] Logical clock integration
- [ ] Tests for all scenarios","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:22:06.196253260Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T06:42:55.317154352Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T06:42:55.317069584Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["distributed","leases","phase4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2c2i","depends_on_id":"bd-1jis","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2c2i","depends_on_id":"bd-3sst","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:09Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3ve9","title":"Implement DeadlineMonitor with warning callbacks","description":"## Overview

Implement the DeadlineMonitor that watches for tasks approaching deadlines without progress and emits warnings.

## DeadlineMonitor Structure

```rust
pub struct DeadlineMonitor {
    /// Configuration for monitoring.
    config: MonitorConfig,
    
    /// Callback for deadline warnings.
    on_warning: Option<Box<dyn Fn(DeadlineWarning) + Send + Sync>>,
    
    /// Tasks being monitored.
    monitored: HashMap<TaskId, MonitoredTask>,
}

#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct MonitorConfig {
    /// How often to check for violations.
    pub check_interval: Duration,
    
    /// Warn if this fraction of deadline remains with no recent progress.
    pub warning_threshold_fraction: f64,  // e.g., 0.1 = warn at 10% remaining
    
    /// Warn if no checkpoint for this duration.
    pub checkpoint_timeout: Duration,
    
    /// Whether monitoring is enabled.
    pub enabled: bool,
}

impl Default for MonitorConfig {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            check_interval: Duration::from_secs(1),
            warning_threshold_fraction: 0.2,  // Warn at 20% remaining
            checkpoint_timeout: Duration::from_secs(30),
            enabled: true,
        }
    }
}

struct MonitoredTask {
    task_id: TaskId,
    region_id: RegionId,
    deadline: Instant,
    last_check: Instant,
    warned: bool,
}
```

## DeadlineWarning

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct DeadlineWarning {
    /// The task approaching its deadline.
    pub task_id: TaskId,
    
    /// The region containing the task.
    pub region_id: RegionId,
    
    /// The absolute deadline.
    pub deadline: Instant,
    
    /// Time remaining until deadline.
    pub remaining: Duration,
    
    /// When the last checkpoint was recorded.
    pub last_checkpoint: Option<Instant>,
    
    /// Message from the last checkpoint.
    pub last_checkpoint_message: Option<String>,
    
    /// Warning reason.
    pub reason: WarningReason,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum WarningReason {
    /// Approaching deadline with little time remaining.
    ApproachingDeadline,
    
    /// No progress (checkpoint) for too long.
    NoProgress,
    
    /// Both approaching deadline AND no recent progress.
    ApproachingDeadlineNoProgress,
}
```

## Monitor Logic

```rust
impl DeadlineMonitor {
    pub fn new(config: MonitorConfig) -> Self;
    
    pub fn on_warning(&mut self, f: impl Fn(DeadlineWarning) + Send + Sync + 'static);
    
    /// Called periodically by the runtime.
    pub fn check(&mut self, tasks: &TaskStore) {
        let now = Instant::now();
        
        for task in tasks.with_deadlines() {
            let remaining = task.deadline.saturating_duration_since(now);
            let threshold = task.deadline_duration * self.config.warning_threshold_fraction;
            
            if remaining <= threshold && !self.already_warned(task.id) {
                self.emit_warning(DeadlineWarning {
                    task_id: task.id,
                    remaining,
                    reason: WarningReason::ApproachingDeadline,
                    // ...
                });
            }
            
            // Check for no-progress
            if let Some(last_cp) = task.last_checkpoint {
                if now.duration_since(last_cp) > self.config.checkpoint_timeout {
                    self.emit_warning(/* ... */);
                }
            }
        }
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] DeadlineMonitor struct with config
- [ ] Warning callback registration
- [ ] check() method for periodic scanning
- [ ] DeadlineWarning with all fields
- [ ] WarningReason variants
- [ ] Warn only once per task (until reset)
- [ ] Unit tests for warning conditions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T18:10:09.548861773Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:38:47.472038152Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T05:38:47.471980313Z","close_reason":"Completed: DeadlineMonitor implemented with tests; full build blocked by transport test compile errors","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ve9","depends_on_id":"bd-nn60","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:01:52Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3z8z","title":"[SUB-EPIC] macOS Reactor (kqueue)","description":"# Sub-Epic: macOS Reactor (kqueue)

## Purpose

Implement a production-quality kqueue-based reactor for macOS and BSD systems. Important for developer experience since many developers use Macs.

## Background

### What is kqueue?

kqueue (kernel event queue) is the BSD/macOS event notification mechanism. Similar to epoll in goals, but different API and semantics.

Key syscalls:
- `kqueue()` - Create kqueue instance
- `kevent()` - Register interest and wait for events (combined operation)

### kqueue vs epoll

| Feature | epoll (Linux) | kqueue (BSD/macOS) |
|---------|---------------|-------------------|
| Level/Edge | Both, flag | Edge by default |
| Add/Wait | Separate calls | Combined in kevent() |
| Multiple events | No | Yes (one call) |
| Timer support | timerfd | Built-in |
| Signal support | signalfd | Built-in |

### Edge-Triggered Behavior

kqueue is edge-triggered by default:
- EV_CLEAR flag re-arms after delivery (like edge-triggered)
- Without EV_CLEAR, event is disabled after delivery (one-shot)

We'll use EV_CLEAR for persistent monitoring.

## Design

```rust
pub struct KqueueReactor {
    /// The kqueue file descriptor
    kq_fd: RawFd,
    /// Token slab and state
    inner: Mutex<KqueueInner>,
}

struct KqueueInner {
    wakers: TokenSlab,
    /// Track registered fds to prevent double-registration
    registrations: HashSet<(RawFd, i16)>, // (fd, filter)
}
```

## Key Differences from Epoll Implementation

1. **Combined register + wait**: kevent() does both
2. **Filters**: EVFILT_READ, EVFILT_WRITE (not flags)
3. **User data**: udata field for token (like epoll_event.data)
4. **Wakeup**: Use pipe or EVFILT_USER (macOS 10.6+)

## Platform Requirements

- macOS 10.6+ (for EVFILT_USER)
- Or FreeBSD, OpenBSD, NetBSD, DragonFlyBSD

## Deliverables

1. KqueueReactor implementing Reactor trait
2. EVFILT_USER wakeup mechanism (or pipe fallback)
3. EV_CLEAR for persistent monitoring
4. Unit tests with real sockets
5. Cross-platform test coverage with Linux

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Feature parity with EpollReactor
- [ ] Works on macOS Ventura+
- [ ] Cross-thread wakeup reliable
- [ ] All epoll tests have kqueue equivalents","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T05:43:22.548330212Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-20T22:15:50.510890807Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T22:15:50.510087003Z","close_reason":"All dependencies complete: wx8h (Core Reactor Abstraction), cysj (register/deregister/poll/wake), and s24w (core KqueueReactor structure) are now CLOSED.  {"id":"bd-41lt","title":"Create Lab reactor determinism tests","description":"# Task: Create Lab Reactor Determinism Tests

## What

Tests that verify the Lab reactor provides deterministic execution, enabling reproducible concurrent tests.

## Location

`tests/lab_determinism.rs` (new file)

## Determinism Principle

Same seed → Same execution → Same results

This is critical for debugging concurrent bugs - if a test fails, the same seed reproduces the exact failure.

## Tests

### Basic Determinism

```rust
#[test]
fn test_lab_deterministic_scheduling() {
    let seed = 42;
    
    // Run twice with same seed
    let result1 = run_with_seed(seed);
    let result2 = run_with_seed(seed);
    
    // Must produce identical results
    assert_eq!(result1, result2);
}

fn run_with_seed(seed: u64) -> Vec<usize> {
    let lab = LabRuntime::new_with_seed(seed);
    let results = Rc::new(RefCell::new(Vec::new()));
    
    lab.block_on(async {
        let results = results.clone();
        
        // Spawn tasks that race
        let tasks: Vec<_> = (0..10).map(|i| {
            let r = results.clone();
            cx.spawn(async move {
                r.borrow_mut().push(i);
            })
        }).collect();
        
        // Wait for all
        for task in tasks {
            task.await;
        }
    });
    
    results.borrow().clone()
}
```

### I/O Event Determinism

```rust
#[test]
fn test_lab_io_event_ordering() {
    let seed = 123;
    
    let result1 = run_io_test(seed);
    let result2 = run_io_test(seed);
    
    assert_eq!(result1, result2);
}

fn run_io_test(seed: u64) -> Vec<String> {
    let lab = LabRuntime::new_with_seed(seed);
    let reactor = lab.reactor();
    let events = Rc::new(RefCell::new(Vec::new()));
    
    lab.block_on(async {
        // Create fake sockets
        let (s1, s2) = create_lab_socket_pair();
        
        // Schedule I/O events
        reactor.schedule_event(s1.token(), Interest::READABLE, Duration::from_millis(10));
        reactor.schedule_event(s2.token(), Interest::READABLE, Duration::from_millis(10));
        
        // Both events at same time - order must be deterministic
        
        // ... capture which fires first ...
    });
    
    events.borrow().clone()
}
```

### Fault Injection Determinism

```rust
#[test]
fn test_lab_fault_injection_determinism() {
    let seed = 456;
    
    // Configure same faults
    let config = FaultConfig {
        read_fail_probability: 0.3,
        error_kind: io::ErrorKind::ConnectionReset,
        ..Default::default()
    };
    
    let failures1 = count_failures_with_seed(seed, config.clone());
    let failures2 = count_failures_with_seed(seed, config);
    
    // Same number of failures, at same operations
    assert_eq!(failures1, failures2);
}
```

### Time Advancement Determinism

```rust
#[test]
fn test_lab_virtual_time_determinism() {
    let seed = 789;
    
    let events1 = run_timed_test(seed);
    let events2 = run_timed_test(seed);
    
    assert_eq!(events1, events2);
}

fn run_timed_test(seed: u64) -> Vec<(Time, String)> {
    let lab = LabRuntime::new_with_seed(seed);
    let events = Rc::new(RefCell::new(Vec::new()));
    
    lab.block_on(async {
        // Spawn timer tasks
        for i in 0..5 {
            let e = events.clone();
            cx.spawn(async move {
                sleep(Duration::from_millis(i * 10)).await;
                e.borrow_mut().push((Cx::current().now(), format!("task-{}", i)));
            });
        }
        
        // Advance time
        lab.advance_time(Duration::from_secs(1));
        lab.run_until_quiescent();
    });
    
    events.borrow().clone()
}
```

### Trace Capture Consistency

```rust
#[test]
fn test_trace_capture_determinism() {
    let seed = 101112;
    
    let trace1 = capture_trace_with_seed(seed);
    let trace2 = capture_trace_with_seed(seed);
    
    // Traces should be identical (same events in same order)
    assert_eq!(trace1.len(), trace2.len());
    for (e1, e2) in trace1.iter().zip(trace2.iter()) {
        assert_eq!(e1, e2);
    }
}
```

### Different Seeds → Different Execution

```rust
#[test]
fn test_different_seeds_different_results() {
    // Different seeds should produce different orderings
    let result1 = run_with_seed(1);
    let result2 = run_with_seed(2);
    let result3 = run_with_seed(3);
    
    // At least some should differ (probabilistically)
    // (If all 10 tasks execute in same order for all seeds, something's wrong)
    let all_same = result1 == result2 && result2 == result3;
    assert!(!all_same, "Different seeds should produce different orderings");
}
```

## Test Utilities

```rust
/// Helper to run test multiple times and verify consistency
fn verify_deterministic<F, T>(seed: u64, runs: usize, f: F)
where
    F: Fn(u64) -> T,
    T: Eq + std::fmt::Debug,
{
    let baseline = f(seed);
    for _ in 1..runs {
        let result = f(seed);
        assert_eq!(result, baseline, "Non-deterministic execution detected");
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Same seed produces same task ordering
- [ ] Same seed produces same I/O event ordering  
- [ ] Same seed produces same fault injection pattern
- [ ] Traces are identical for same seed
- [ ] Different seeds produce different execution
- [ ] Virtual time advancement is deterministic
- [ ] Tests document any sources of non-determinism","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ClaudeOpus","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T05:51:26.148849595Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-27T21:03:06.042536223Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T21:03:06.042460052Z","close_reason":"Lab reactor determinism tests implemented in tests/lab_determinism.rs (seed, IO order, fault injection, trace, time)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-41lt","depends_on_id":"bd-7tk3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:01:52Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-rv2","title":"[Sync] Implement Cancel-Aware RwLock","description":"# Cancel-Aware RwLock

## Overview
Async read-write lock with multiple readers OR one writer, cancel-aware.

## Core Type

```rust
pub struct RwLock<T: ?Sized> {
    // State encoding:
    // - Bits 0-29: reader count (max ~1 billion)
    // - Bit 30: write locked
    // - Bit 31: write pending (for fairness)
    state: AtomicU32,
    writer_waker: AtomicWaker,
    reader_waiters: WaiterQueue,
    data: UnsafeCell<T>,
}

const WRITE_LOCKED: u32 = 1 << 30;
const WRITE_PENDING: u32 = 1 << 31;
const READER_MASK: u32 = (1 << 30) - 1;

impl<T> RwLock<T> {
    pub const fn new(value: T) -> Self;
    pub fn into_inner(self) -> T;
}

impl<T: ?Sized> RwLock<T> {
    /// Acquire read lock
    pub async fn read(&self) -> RwLockReadGuard<'_, T>;
    
    /// Try read lock
    pub fn try_read(&self) -> Option<RwLockReadGuard<'_, T>>;
    
    /// Acquire write lock
    pub async fn write(&self) -> RwLockWriteGuard<'_, T>;
    
    /// Try write lock
    pub fn try_write(&self) -> Option<RwLockWriteGuard<'_, T>>;
    
    /// Get mutable reference
    pub fn get_mut(&mut self) -> &mut T;
}
```

## Guards

```rust
pub struct RwLockReadGuard<'a, T: ?Sized> {
    lock: &'a RwLock<T>,
}

impl<T: ?Sized> Deref for RwLockReadGuard<'_, T> {
    type Target = T;
    fn deref(&self) -> &T {
        unsafe { &*self.lock.data.get() }
    }
}

impl<T: ?Sized> Drop for RwLockReadGuard<'_, T> {
    fn drop(&mut self) {
        let old = self.lock.state.fetch_sub(1, Ordering::Release);
        // If last reader and writer pending, wake writer
        if old & READER_MASK == 1 && old & WRITE_PENDING != 0 {
            self.lock.writer_waker.wake();
        }
    }
}

pub struct RwLockWriteGuard<'a, T: ?Sized> {
    lock: &'a RwLock<T>,
}

impl<T: ?Sized> Deref for RwLockWriteGuard<'_, T> {
    type Target = T;
    fn deref(&self) -> &T {
        unsafe { &*self.lock.data.get() }
    }
}

impl<T: ?Sized> DerefMut for RwLockWriteGuard<'_, T> {
    fn deref_mut(&mut self) -> &mut T {
        unsafe { &mut *self.lock.data.get() }
    }
}

impl<T: ?Sized> Drop for RwLockWriteGuard<'_, T> {
    fn drop(&mut self) {
        self.lock.state.fetch_and(!WRITE_LOCKED, Ordering::Release);
        // Wake all waiting readers
        self.lock.reader_waiters.wake_all();
    }
}
```

## Owned Guards

```rust
pub struct OwnedRwLockReadGuard<T: ?Sized> {
    lock: Arc<RwLock<T>>,
}

pub struct OwnedRwLockWriteGuard<T: ?Sized> {
    lock: Arc<RwLock<T>>,
}
```

## Upgradable Read Guard (Optional)

```rust
pub struct RwLockUpgradableReadGuard<'a, T: ?Sized> {
    lock: &'a RwLock<T>,
}

impl<'a, T: ?Sized> RwLockUpgradableReadGuard<'a, T> {
    /// Upgrade to write lock
    pub async fn upgrade(self) -> RwLockWriteGuard<'a, T>;
    
    /// Try to upgrade
    pub fn try_upgrade(self) -> Result<RwLockWriteGuard<'a, T>, Self>;
    
    /// Downgrade to read lock
    pub fn downgrade(self) -> RwLockReadGuard<'a, T>;
}
```

## Fairness Policy
- Write-preferring: pending writers block new readers
- Prevents writer starvation
- WRITE_PENDING bit signals to readers

## Cancel-Safety
- read(): cancel removes from queue
- write(): cancel removes from queue
- Guards always release on drop

## Testing
- Multiple readers
- Exclusive writer
- Writer blocks readers
- Reader blocks writers
- Upgrade/downgrade
- Cancel during acquisition

 {"id":"bd-rww6","title":"WebSocket client: Cx-integrated connect and message passing","description":"# WebSocket Client Implementation

## Overview
Implements the WebSocket client for outbound connections with full Cx integration,
structured concurrency, and cancel-correctness.

## Background & Motivation
WebSocket is the standard for full-duplex communication. Any Tokio user who says
'I can use tokio-tungstenite' must be met with 'We have a superior, cancel-correct
alternative with structured concurrency guarantees.'

## Key Design Points

### Cx Integration
- connect() establishes WebSocket connection over TCP or TLS
- send()/recv() work with Cx checkpoints
- Cancellation triggers clean close with code 1001
- Ping/pong handled automatically
- Configuration for max sizes, compression, keepalive

### Cancellation Semantics
- When Cx is cancelled, client sends Close frame with code 1001 (Going Away)
- Close handshake is best-effort with short timeout
- Stream is dropped cleanly after close or timeout
- No data loss: pending sends are flushed before close attempt

## Dependencies
- Requires: WebSocket frame codec (bd-r4hu)
- Requires: WebSocket handshake (bd-1kg0)
- Blocks: WebSocket E2E tests

- [ ] Cancellation triggers clean close with code 1001
- [ ] Ping/pong handled automatically
- [ ] Configuration for max sizes, compression, keepalive
- [ ] Unit tests for connection lifecycle

 {"id":"bd-s24w","title":"Implement KqueueReactor core structure","description":"# Task: Implement KqueueReactor Core Structure

## What

Create the KqueueReactor struct with kqueue file descriptor management and basic lifecycle.

## Location

`src/runtime/reactor/kqueue.rs` (new file)

## Design

```rust
use std::os::unix::io::RawFd;
use std::sync::Mutex;

/// Kqueue-based reactor for macOS and BSD.
///
/// Uses EV_CLEAR for edge-triggered behavior.
/// Thread-safe via internal synchronization.
#[cfg(any(target_os = "macos", target_os = "freebsd", target_os = "openbsd", target_os = "netbsd"))]
pub struct KqueueReactor {
    /// The kqueue file descriptor
    kq_fd: RawFd,
    /// Pipe for cross-thread wakeup (EVFILT_USER only on macOS)
    wake_pipe: (RawFd, RawFd), // (read, write)
    /// Internal state
    inner: Mutex<KqueueInner>,
}

struct KqueueInner {
    wakers: TokenSlab,
    /// Track (fd, filter) to prevent double-registration
    registrations: HashMap<Token, (RawFd, FilterSet)>,
}

#[derive(Copy, Clone)]
struct FilterSet {
    read: bool,
    write: bool,
}

impl KqueueReactor {
    pub fn new() -> io::Result<Self> {
        // Create kqueue
        let kq_fd = unsafe { libc::kqueue() };
        if kq_fd < 0 {
            return Err(io::Error::last_os_error());
        }
        
        // Create wake pipe
        let mut fds = [0i32; 2];
        if unsafe { libc::pipe(fds.as_mut_ptr()) } < 0 {
            unsafe { libc::close(kq_fd); }
            return Err(io::Error::last_os_error());
        }
        
        // Make wake pipe non-blocking
        for fd in &fds {
            let flags = unsafe { libc::fcntl(*fd, libc::F_GETFL) };
            unsafe { libc::fcntl(*fd, libc::F_SETFL, flags | libc::O_NONBLOCK); }
        }
        
        let reactor = Self {
            kq_fd,
            wake_pipe: (fds[0], fds[1]),
            inner: Mutex::new(KqueueInner {
                wakers: TokenSlab::new(),
                registrations: HashMap::new(),
            }),
        };
        
        // Register wake pipe read end with kqueue
        reactor.register_wake_pipe()?;
        
        Ok(reactor)
    }
    
    fn register_wake_pipe(&self) -> io::Result<()> {
        let kev = libc::kevent {
            ident: self.wake_pipe.0 as usize,
            filter: libc::EVFILT_READ,
            flags: libc::EV_ADD | libc::EV_CLEAR,
            fflags: 0,
            data: 0,
            udata: std::ptr::null_mut(), // Special token for wake
        };
        
        let ret = unsafe {
            libc::kevent(self.kq_fd, &kev, 1, std::ptr::null_mut(), 0, std::ptr::null())
        };
        
        if ret < 0 {
            return Err(io::Error::last_os_error());
        }
        Ok(())
    }
}

impl Drop for KqueueReactor {
    fn drop(&mut self) {
        unsafe {
            libc::close(self.kq_fd);
            libc::close(self.wake_pipe.0);
            libc::close(self.wake_pipe.1);
        }
    }
}
```

## Why Pipe Instead of EVFILT_USER

While macOS supports EVFILT_USER for user-triggered events:
- More portable (works on all BSDs)
- Simpler to reason about
- Pipe semantics well understood

Could optimize later with EVFILT_USER if needed.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] KqueueReactor struct defined
- [ ] new() creates kqueue instance
- [ ] Wake pipe created and registered
- [ ] Drop closes all file descriptors
- [ ] Only compiles on macOS/BSD (cfg attribute)

 {"id":"bd-s4hw","title":"Write property tests for region tree","description":"## Overview

Write the actual property tests that use proptest to verify region tree invariants under random operation sequences.

## Test Harness

```rust
/// Test harness that wraps a region tree and tracks created entities.
pub struct TestHarness {
    tree: RegionTree,
    regions: Vec<RegionId>,
    tasks: Vec<TaskId>,
    rng: StdRng,
}

impl TestHarness {
    pub fn new(seed: u64) -> Self {
        let mut tree = RegionTree::new();
        let root = tree.root_id();
        
        Self {
            tree,
            regions: vec![root],
            tasks: Vec::new(),
            rng: StdRng::seed_from_u64(seed),
        }
    }
    
    /// Resolve a selector to an actual RegionId.
    pub fn resolve_region(&self, selector: &RegionSelector) -> Option<RegionId> {
        self.regions.get(selector.0 % self.regions.len()).copied()
    }
    
    /// Apply an operation and check invariants.
    pub fn apply_and_check(&mut self, op: &RegionOp) {
        op.apply(self);
        assert_all_invariants(&self.tree);
    }
}
```

## Property Tests

### Test 1: Random Operations Preserve Structure
```rust
proptest! {
    #![proptest_config(ProptestConfig::with_cases(10000))]
    
    #[test]
    fn random_operations_preserve_invariants(
        seed: u64,
        ops in prop::collection::vec(any::<RegionOp>(), 0..500)
    ) {
        let mut harness = TestHarness::new(seed);
        
        for op in &ops {
            harness.apply_and_check(op);
        }
    }
}
```

### Test 2: Deep Nesting Stress Test
```rust
proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn deep_nesting_maintains_invariants(depth in 1usize..200) {
        let mut harness = TestHarness::new(42);
        
        // Create a very deep tree
        let mut current = harness.tree.root_id();
        for _ in 0..depth {
            current = harness.create_child(current);
            assert_all_invariants(&harness.tree);
        }
        
        // Cancel from root, verify propagation
        harness.tree.cancel(harness.tree.root_id(), CancelKind::User);
        assert_all_invariants(&harness.tree);
    }
}
```

### Test 3: Wide Tree Stress Test
```rust
proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn wide_tree_maintains_invariants(width in 1usize..500) {
        let mut harness = TestHarness::new(42);
        let root = harness.tree.root_id();
        
        // Create many children at root level
        for _ in 0..width {
            harness.create_child(root);
            assert_all_invariants(&harness.tree);
        }
    }
}
```

### Test 4: Cancellation Propagation
```rust
proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn cancellation_always_propagates(
        seed: u64,
        setup_ops in prop::collection::vec(any::<RegionOp>(), 10..100),
        cancel_target: RegionSelector
    ) {
        let mut harness = TestHarness::new(seed);
        
        // Build a tree
        for op in &setup_ops {
            harness.apply_and_check(op);
        }
        
        // Cancel a random region
        if let Some(target) = harness.resolve_region(&cancel_target) {
            harness.tree.cancel(target, CancelKind::User);
            assert_all_invariants(&harness.tree);
            
            // Verify all descendants cancelled
            for desc in harness.tree.descendants(target) {
                prop_assert!(harness.tree.get_region(desc).unwrap().is_cancelled());
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### Test 5: Full Lifecycle Test
```rust
proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn full_lifecycle_preserves_invariants(
        seed: u64,
        create_ops in prop::collection::vec(any::<RegionOp>(), 50..200),
        destroy_ops in prop::collection::vec(any::<RegionOp>(), 50..200)
    ) {
        let mut harness = TestHarness::new(seed);
        
        // Build up
        for op in &create_ops {
            harness.apply_and_check(op);
        }
        
        // Tear down
        for op in &destroy_ops {
            harness.apply_and_check(op);
        }
        
        // Full shutdown
        harness.tree.shutdown();
        assert_all_invariants(&harness.tree);
    }
}
```
